capture._doc: How committed source/realworld_capture.json evidence is consumed. use_elevation applies the captured road-surface altitude to the centerline Y. evidence.corrected_elevation now reins in the phone's GPS/baro noise: it rejects transient spikes (the phone physically fell on Sand Creek ~idx 1240), gates corrections to ~0 on near-flat terrain, and permits a metre-scale lift ONLY inside a declared 'bridges' span. So off a bridge the road follows the accurate USGS 3DEP heightfield (the old fake bumps on 56th/45th/Sand Creek and the N Ash drop collapse to sub-metre); at a real crossing the level deck is preserved. Captured TEXTURES and lateral evidence are unaffected. 'bridges' = [{center_m, len_m}] in lap-station metres (same convention as road_profile.dips) — the ONLY genuine deck crossings. The route crosses the ACTUAL Sand Creek twice (0.9 m from the mapped creek line both times): E 56th over Sand Creek (station ~2485; bare earth dips ~7 m to the creek bed, the LOWEST point on the route — the deck stays level on the approach to the corkscrew turn) and Quebec over Sand Creek (station ~5950; bare earth dips ~6 m). Both also get visual bridge structure (parapets/piers) from build_env._creek_bridge.

road_text._doc: Big painted street-name decals at each turn-onto. mode 'along' = lengthwise down the lane (drive over it, head-tilt to read); 'across' = sign-flat facing the driver (wraps the road type to a 2nd line). cap_m = letter height across the road. flip_read/flip_up mirror the axes if it reads backwards in-game. min_leg_m = shortest street leg that gets a label.

road_profile._doc: Corkscrew dips/cambers REMOVED — Sand Creek elevation now comes from real USGS 3DEP + capture (user: the hand-authored corkscrew sucks). Re-add dips/cambers here to bring it back.

road_edge._doc: Urban street edge swept along every road: road -> raised curb -> flat sidewalk -> graded grass as ONE continuous profile sharing seam vertices with the road and the grass, so nothing hovers (scripts/geometry/ribbon.py:curb_sidewalk; physical 1KERB_sidewalk). Omit (or profile != 'sidewalk') for the default asphalt shoulder. curb_h/sidewalk_w/grade_w in metres.

scenery._doc: Per-track foliage/scatter knobs read by scripts/environment/build_env.py. Defaults (shown) reproduce the original look; override per track (e.g. a desert track wants sparser, lower scrub). corridor_margin_m keeps ALL scatter this far clear of every road (main loop + connectors).

signs._doc: Street-name blade signs at each major junction (from data/street_labels.json, same legs as the painted road decals). enabled=false drops them. flip_read/flip_up are one-bit fixes if the text reads mirrored or upside-down in-game (verify on the first Windows drive).

layouts._doc: Outer loop: Colorado -> 50th -> Dahlia -> 56th -> Sand Creek -> Quebec -> 45th -> 46th -> start. The 'interior' layout was dropped: its interior_grid connector was never defined, and layouts share one kn5 so a distinct interior circuit isn't achievable without splitting geometry.

kerb._about: Racing-circuit kerbs: taller + rideable ramp on the lane side (vs the default 5cm street kerb).
